---
title: "Cannot boot Edgy using ATI 7200 with DRI enabled"
date: 2006-11-05
forum: Multimedia &amp; Video
---

### Post by barney_1 on 2006-11-05
Hi All,

I upgraded from Dapper to Edgy this week.  I have an ATI Radeon 7200 video card but when I tried to boot the first time, I had a hard freeze at the logon screen.  If I disable the DRI module I can get in, but then there is no 3d accel.  Can anyone shed some light on the situation for me?

Thanks!

xorg.conf
```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
```

bump

### Post by sikee on 2006-11-08
I am experiencing the same issue.  See this thread for more info: [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=292906]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=292906") , no rhesolution yet but seeing that I can't successfully boot the LiveCD it would seem that the problem is in Edgy, not my config.

---

### Post by lazyart on 2006-11-08
I have 7200 on Dapper.  I removed fglrx, installed open source, then installed fglrx.  Ok, it wasnt that "simple" (some editing of xorg.conf necessary) but that enabled direct rendering.

Don't know if that will help any. Hopefully it will.

---

### Post by barney_1 on 2006-11-08
Now we're making some progress.  Following CarpKing's advice I changed the device section of my xorg.conf to reflect this:

```
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R100 QD [Radeon 7200]"
	Driver "ati"
	BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
	Option "DRI"     "true"
	Option "AGPMode" "8" 
	Option "AGPSize" "64"
	#Option "RingSize" "8"
	Option "BufferSize" "2"
	#Option "UseFBDev" "true"
	Option "AccelMethod" "XAA"
	Option "XaaNoOffscreenPixmaps" "false"
	#Option "RenderAccel" "true" 
	Option "BackingStore" "true" 
	Option "EnablePageFlip" "true"
	Option "ColorTiling" "true"
	Option "DPMS"
	#Option "AGPFastWrite" "1"
EndSection
```

and my /etc/modules to:
```
# /etc/modules: kernel modules to load at boot time.
#
# This file contains the names of kernel modules that should be loaded
# at boot time, one per line. Lines beginning with "#" are ignored.


lp
psmouse
sbp2
sr_mod
amd64_agp
drm
agpgart
radeon
```

The good news is I don't get the lock up anymore, the bad news is I don't have dri working either.  This is from my Xorg.0.log and I think it's the culprit:

```
(II) RADEON(0): [drm] DRM interface version 1.2
(II) RADEON(0): [drm] created "radeon" driver at busid "pci:0000:01:00.0"
(II) RADEON(0): [drm] added 8192 byte SAREA at 0xf8d02000
(II) RADEON(0): [drm] mapped SAREA 0xf8d02000 to 0xb7ad8000
(II) RADEON(0): [drm] framebuffer handle = 0xf0000000
(II) RADEON(0): [drm] added 1 reserved context for kernel
(II) RADEON(0): [agp] Mode 0x1f000201 [AGP 0x1106/0x3188; Card 0x1002/0x5144]
(II) RADEON(0): [agp] 65536 kB allocated with handle 0x00000001
(EE) RADEON(0): [agp] Could not bind
(EE) RADEON(0): [agp] AGP failed to initialize. Disabling the DRI.
(II) RADEON(0): [agp] You may want to make sure the agpgart kernel module
is loaded before the radeon kernel module.
(**) RADEON(0): RADEONDRICloseScreen
(II) RADEON(0): [drm] removed 1 reserved context for kernel
(II) RADEON(0): [drm] unmapping 8192 bytes of SAREA 0xf8d02000 at 0xb7ad8000
(**) RADEON(0): RADEONInitMemoryMap() : 
(**) RADEON(0):   mem_size         : 0x04000000
(**) RADEON(0):   MC_FB_LOCATION   : 0xf3fff000
(**) RADEON(0):   MC_AGP_LOCATION  : 0xffffffc0
(**) RADEON(0): RADEONModeInit()
1024x768       94.50  1024 1072 1168 1376   768  769  772  808 (24,32) +H +V
1024x768       94.50  1024 1072 1168 1376   768  769  772  808 (24,32) +H +V
(**) RADEON(0): Pitch = 8388736 bytes (virtualX = 1024, displayWidth = 1024)

(**) RADEON(0): RADEONInit returns 0x81fe7f0

(**) RADEON(0):   MC_FB_LOCATION   : 0xf3fff000
```

---

### Post by CarpKing on 2006-11-08
That's odd... using my fix, you come up with the same error that I started with...

Keep in mind that the amd64_agp module is only for the AMD 64 chipset.  If you have a different processor, you would use a different module in its place.  That said, I still haven't determined if the /etc/modules lines are necessary in my case.  

Please post the output of:
```
dmesg | grep agp
```

We'll see if we can figure this out.

---

```
[17179588.636000] Linux agpgart interface v0.101 (c) Dave Jones
```

---

### Post by CarpKing on 2006-11-09
Ok, that is along the lines of what I expected, which is a good sign I think.  The "legacy mode" business still worries me a bit, but for now try changing "AGPSize" from "64" to "32".  When I was getting the "could not bind" error, I had AGPSize set to 256, but my dmesg said the aperture was 64M.  I hope this fixes your problem.  You might also want to look into changing or commenting out AGPMode.  My card is a 9600, so maybe that's what the "legacy mode" thing is about.

---

### Post by barney_1 on 2006-11-10
SOLVED!

It appears I was missing a link that I needed to boot with dri enabled.  Thanks to [suloku's post]("http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1354006&postcount=75") I creaded a link with this line of code:
```
sudo ln -s /usr/lib/dri /usr/lib/xorg/modules/dri
```
Now I get this from glxinfo | grep rendering:
```
glxinfo | grep rendering
libGL warning: 3D driver claims to not support visual 0x4b
direct rendering: Yes

```
Yes, there's a warning, but dri is working! Thanks everyone for the help you have given me!

---

### Post by CarpKing on 2006-11-10
Glad I could be of help.  Not sure what the deal with that error is; seems that a lot of people (including myself) get that.  Doesn't seem to cause any problems in and of itself, though.

### Post by barney_1 on 2006-11-12
Well crap, my problem is back again.  Although I got the system working and restarted several times, when I shutdown and rebooted the next day my system had the same hard freeze. 

Any ideas why an shutdown would react differently than a reboot?

---

### Post by barney_1 on 2006-11-12
I'm at my wits end here.  I can get dri working intermittently.  Once working, I can reboot all I want and dri still works but if I shutdown (and everything powers off) I get a hard freeze at the login screen.  The biggest frustration is that I can't reproduce the methods I've use to get acceleration working.

Is it possible that I have a faulty video card?  Is there a way to test this?

---

### Post by barney_1 on 2006-11-12
ok, after much trial and error I think I've pinpointed how I'm getting edgy to boot with DRI.  I run a dual boot system with Edgy as my main OS and winXP for a few programs that I can't run with Wine.  It seems that if I boot to winXP, choose Reboot from the shutdown menu, then boot to edgy I dri works no problem.

So my theory now is that the winXP driver puts my video card in a mode that is receptive to dri, when I reboot the power to the card is not cut so it stays in that mode.

Does that sound plausable and how can I fix it so that I don't need to boot windows to get edgy's dri working?

---

### Post by MacS on 2007-02-04
Anyone figure this out yet?

---

### Post by barney_1 on 2007-02-05
Why yes, I did figure it out.  Shame on me for not posting an answer when I did (months ago,  I'm no long using that card actually).

My problem was fixed with a bios update.  I could have sworn I had the most recent version, but I didn't.  I updated the bios and on the next boot DRI loaded and I was able to use direct rendering.

---
